zh.lifeSummary: 當James de Clifford於大約1564年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福時，他的父親Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford29歲，母親Joan Beard25歲。他於1612年與Blanche Hopkins在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾斯文頓 維利亞結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1638年在英國格盧斯特斯爾斯文頓 維利亞享年75歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾斯文頓 維利亞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James de Clifford于大约1564年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾福藍普頓 奧 澤福时，他的父亲Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford29岁，母亲Joan Beard25岁。他于1612年与Blanche Hopkins在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾斯文頓 維利亞结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1638年在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯文頓 維利亞享年75岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯文頓 維利亞。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When James de Clifford was born about 1564, in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England, his father, Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford, was 29 and his mother, Joan Beard, was 25. He married Blanche Hopkins in 1612, in Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1638, in Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 75, and was buried in Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Eng: Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque James de Clifford est né environ 1564, à Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Joan Beard, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Blanche Hopkins en 1612, à Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1638, à Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als James de Clifford um 1564 in Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford 29 und seine Mutter Joan Beard 25. Er hat 1612 in Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Blanche Hopkins geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1638 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, England und wurde in Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

Lui sposato Blanche Hopkins nel 1612, nel Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: James de Cliffordが約1564年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアフランプトン オン セバーンで生まれた時，父Wyllm Clyfford Clyffordは29歳，母Joan Beardは25歳であった。1612年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスウィンドン ヴィラッジでBlanche Hopkinsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1638年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスウィンドン ヴィラッジで75歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスウィンドン ヴィラッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: James de Clifford(이)가 대략 1564년 영국 그루스스터실르 프램프턴  세버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Beard(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1612년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 스윈던 비라지에서 Blanche Hopkins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1638년 영국 그루스스터실르 스윈던 비라지에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 스윈던 비라지에 묻혔다.

pt.Of, Swindon, Gloucestershire, England: Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando James de Clifford nasceu aproximadamente 1564, em Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Joan Beard, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Blanche Hopkins em 1612, em Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1638, em Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Eng: фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда James de Clifford родился около 1564 г. в местности фрамптон он сивирн, глоусестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Beard, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Blanche Hopkins в 1612 г. в местности свиндон вилладж, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1638 г. в местности свиндон вилладж, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности свиндон вилладж, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.Of, Swindon, Gloucestershire, England: Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando James de Clifford nació aproximadamente 1564, en Frampton on Severn, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Wyllm Clyfford Clyfford, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Joan Beard, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Blanche Hopkins en 1612, en Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1638, en Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Swindon Village, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
